title: श्री महाकाल स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री महाकाल स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय महाकालेश्वराय।
उज्जयिनीपुरवासाय कालाय नमो नमः॥

महाकालं महादेवं कालजिता जगत्पतिम्।
उज्जयिन्यां समासीनं प्रणमामि सदाशिवम्॥ १॥

कालचक्रप्रवर्तकं कालकालं महेश्वरम्।
मृत्युञ्जयं त्र्यम्बकं देवं महाकालं नमाम्यहम्॥ २॥

शिप्रातीरे विमले क्षेत्रे अवन्तिकायां सुपुण्यदे।
ज्योतिर्लिङ्गं महापुण्यं महाकालेश्वरं भजे॥ ३॥

भस्मोद्धूलितसर्वाङ्गं नागयज्ञोपवीतिनम्।
रुद्राक्षमालाभरणं महाकालं नमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

त्रिशूलडमरुधारिणं नीलकण्ठं कपालिनम्।
ज्वालामालिनमीशानं महाकालं भजाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

भैरवानन्ददं देवं भस्मप्रियं कपर्दिनम्।
श्मशानवासिनं शान्तं महाकालं नमाम्यहम्॥ ६॥

कालस्य कालं कालज्ञं कालचक्रप्रवर्तकम्।
कालात्मकं कालरूपं महाकालं भजाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

सन्ध्याकाले महादेवो भस्मारत्या सुशोभितः।
नृत्यते भूतवृन्दैश्च महाकालो विराजते॥ ८॥

दक्षिणे मार्गतो यत्र हरसिद्धिर्विराजते।
शक्तिसमन्वितः शम्भुः महाकालो जगद्गुरुः॥ ९॥

राज्ञो विक्रमसेनस्य पालकः पुरुषोत्तमः।
उज्जयिन्या सदा रक्षा महाकालः करोति हि॥ १०॥

कालभैरवसेवितं सिद्धसाधकसेवितम्।
योगिनीगणसंयुक्तं महाकालं नमाम्यहम्॥ ११॥

महाकालस्य या पूजा उज्जयिन्यां विशेषतः।
तस्य स्तोत्रस्य पाठेन तत्फलं समवाप्नुयात्॥ १२॥

॥ इति श्री महाकाल स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Mahakal Stotram ||

Om Namah Shivaya Mahakaleshwaraya |
Ujjayini-pura-vasaya Kalaya namo namah ||

Mahakalam Mahadevam Kalajita Jagatpatim |
Ujjayinyam samasinam pranamami Sadashivam || 1 ||

Kalachakra-pravartakam Kalakalam Maheshwaram |
Mrityunjayam Tryambakam Devam Mahakalam namamyaham || 2 ||

Shipra-tire vimale kshetre Avantikayam su-punyade |
Jyotirlingam maha-punyam Mahakaleshwaram bhaje || 3 ||

Bhasmod-dhulita-sarvangam naga-yajnopaveetinam |
Rudraksha-mala-bharanam Mahakalam namamyaham || 4 ||

Trishula-damaru-dharinam Nilakantham Kapalinam |
Jvala-malinam-Ishanam Mahakalam bhajamyaham || 5 ||

Bhairavanandadam Devam bhasma-priyam Kapardinam |
Shmashana-vasinam shantam Mahakalam namamyaham || 6 ||

Kalasya Kalam Kalajnam Kalachakra-pravartakam |
Kalatmakam Kalarupam Mahakalam bhajamyaham || 7 ||

Sandhya-kale Mahadevo bhasmaratya sushobhitah |
Nrityate bhuta-vrindaish-cha Mahakalo virajate || 8 ||

Dakshine margato yatra Harasiddhir-virajate |
Shakti-samanvitah Shambhuh Mahakalo Jagadguruh || 9 ||

Rajno Vikramasenasya palakah Purushottamah |
Ujjayinya sada raksha Mahakalah karoti hi || 10 ||

Kalabhairava-sevitam Siddha-sadhaka-sevitam |
Yogini-gana-samyuktam Mahakalam namamyaham || 11 ||

Mahakalasya ya puja Ujjayinyam visheshatah |
Tasya stotrasya pathena tat-phalam samavapnuyat || 12 ||

|| Iti Shri Mahakal Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: उज्जयिनी में विराजमान, काल को भी जीतने वाले, जगत्पति, सदाशिव महाकाल महादेव को प्रणाम। कालचक्र के प्रवर्तक, काल के भी काल, मृत्युञ्जय, त्र्यम्बक महाकाल को नमस्कार। शिप्रा नदी के तट पर अवन्तिका क्षेत्र में ज्योतिर्लिंग रूप महाकालेश्वर को भजता हूँ। भस्म से विभूषित, नागयज्ञोपवीती, रुद्राक्षमाला धारी, त्रिशूल-डमरू धारी, नीलकण्ठ, कपाली, ज्वालामाली, भैरवानन्द देने वाले, श्मशानवासी, शान्त — ऐसे महाकाल को नमस्कार। सन्ध्याकाल में भस्मारती से सुशोभित, भूतगणों सहित नृत्य करते हुए महाकाल विराजते हैं। राजा विक्रमसेन (विक्रमादित्य) के पालक, उज्जयिनी के सदा रक्षक महाकाल हैं।
benefits: Conquers the fear of death (Mrityunjaya aspect)
Equivalent to pilgrimage to Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants liberation
Traditionally invoked for protection from untimely death and accidents
Invokes victory over time and all obstacles
Invokes the blessing of the fearless grace of bhairava
description: The Mahakal Stotram, known in Sanskrit as श्री महाकाल स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Shiva. This revered text originates from Shiva Purana. Regular recitation is believed to destroy all sins and grant liberation, grant victory over time and all obstacles, and protect from untimely death and accidents. This prayer is traditionally recited on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan Somvar to invoke divine grace. The text contains 12 verses and takes around 9 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Mahakal Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Mahakal Stotram in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga at Ujjain for protection.

seoFaqs.question: Which Jyotirlinga is the Mahakal Stotram dedicated to?
seoFaqs.answer: The Mahakal Stotram is dedicated to Shiva enshrined as Mahakaleshwar at the Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, situated on the banks of the Shipra river. This is one of the twelve canonical Jyotirlingas listed in the Shiva Purana, and the only Jyotirlinga in the country that is dakshina-mukhi (south-facing), which has special tantric significance because the south is the direction of Yama, the lord of death. The stotram invokes Shiva specifically as the conqueror of time and death, addressing him with the epithets Mahakala, Avantika-natha, Kala-kala and Mrityunjaya, all of which are tied to the geography and theology of Ujjain rather than to Shiva in general.

seoFaqs.question: What does the name 'Mahakal' specifically mean in this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: 'Mahakal' is a compound of maha (great) and kala (time, and by extension death). In the stotram, the epithet works on three levels. Cosmologically, it identifies Shiva as the totality that contains and dissolves time itself, beyond past, present and future. Theologically, it names him as the one who is the death of death (kala-kala), invoked for protection from akala-mrityu, untimely death. Geographically, it ties him to Ujjain, which Puranic tradition identifies as the navel of the earth and the prime meridian of ancient Indian time-keeping (the Tropic of Cancer once passed through Ujjain). The stotram weaves these three layers, which is why a generic Shiva stotram cannot substitute for it.

seoFaqs.question: What is special about the Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga that the stotram references?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram alludes to several features unique to this shrine. First, the linga is swayambhu (self-manifested) rather than installed by a human, a status it shares with only a small subset of the twelve Jyotirlingas. Second, it is the only Jyotirlinga that receives the Bhasma Aarti at brahma muhurta (around 4 am), where ash from cremation grounds (now substituted with cow-dung ash) is offered to the linga, dramatising Shiva's mastery over death. Third, it is dakshina-mukhi, allowing the deity to face the realm of Yama directly. Fourth, the temple stands in the kshetra of King Vikramaditya, whose era forms the basis of the Vikram Samvat calendar still used in Hindu panchangs.

seoFaqs.question: What boons does the Mahakal Stotram traditionally promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti and accompanying tradition emphasise two principal boons: mukti (liberation) and protection from akala-mrityu (untimely death). The Puranic narrative connecting Mahakal to the asura Dushana, who terrorised the brahmins of Avantika, frames Mahakaleshwar as a protector deity who manifested specifically to shield his devotees from premature death and adharmic forces. Additional benefits commonly cited include relief from Saturn-related afflictions (Sade Sati, dhaiyya), removal of pitru dosha through pinda-daan at the adjoining Siddhavat ghat, and success in legal and royal matters because of the temple's historical association with Vikramaditya's court. These specific anchors distinguish it from a generic Shiva phala-shruti.

seoFaqs.question: When is the Mahakal Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The most prescribed times are during the Bhasma Aarti at brahma muhurta, on every Monday (Somvar) and Pradosha tithi, on Shravan Somvars when Ujjain hosts the famous savari procession of Mahakal through the city, on Maha Shivratri when the temple stays open for 44 hours, and on Naga Panchami when the temple's underground Nagchandreshwar shrine opens once a year. Devotees performing pitru-tarpana at Siddhavat or seeking Kaal Sarp Dosh shanti often recite the stotram alongside the Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra. For home parayana, the standard practice is to chant it daily at dawn or dusk facing south, with bilva leaves and water offered to a Shiva linga.
